# Originary | Prove what agents did

> Originary builds production surfaces for verification and evidence across AI agent, API, MCP, and automated interaction workflows. Verify requests, apply policy, and return signed records teams can prove later across vendors, audits, disputes, and reviews. PEAC is the open standard underneath. Self-hostable. Offline-verifiable. No lock-in.

Last updated: 2026-04-09

Company: Poem, Inc.
Location: Delaware, USA
Contact: contact@originary.xyz

## Overview

- [Home](/) - Prove what agents did. AI agent verification with signed, portable audit trails.
- [Products](/products) - Originary Verify, Agent Auditor, Gateway 402, MCP Server, Trace
- [PEAC Protocol](/peac) - Open standard for verifiable interaction records
- [Developers](/developers) - Quickstart, SDKs, and integration guides
- [Downloads](/downloads) - CLI, MCP server, SDKs, and source archives
- [Proof Check](/agent-proof-check) - Paste logs, traces, or signed records. See what is observable, verifiable, and missing.
- [Agent Auditor](/agent-auditor) - Open and verify any signed record locally, with evidence bundles
- [Trust Center](/trust) - Verification model, cryptography, data boundaries, portability
- [Enterprise](/enterprise) - Deployment options, security model, data boundaries, procurement evidence
- [Pricing](/pricing) - OSS only, supported self-host, or managed verification

## Protocol State

- Stable release: v0.12.7 (latest, Apr 8 2026)
- Wire format: interaction-record+jwt (Wire 0.2, current stable)
- Legacy wire format: peac-receipt/0.1 (Wire 0.1, frozen)
- Published packages: 35 on npm (@peac/*)
- Tests: 7241
- Conformance requirement IDs: 219
- Build targets: 96
- License: Apache-2.0
- Node.js: >=22.0.0

## Products

### Originary Verify (/products/verify)
The fastest way to operationalize PEAC in production. Validate signatures, key material, policy binding, and evidence exports at scale. Hosted or self-hosted deployment paths. Enterprise evidence exports.

### Agent Auditor (/agent-auditor)
Open any signed record file (.jws, .json, .zip) and inspect its contents. Verifies Ed25519 signatures locally. No signup, no backend, no data leaves your machine. Apache-2.0.

### Gateway 402 (/products/gateway-402)
Enforce access and payment policy at the edge. Issue signed records on every response. HTTP 402 challenge flows.

### MCP Server (/integrations/mcp)
MCP tool server (@peac/mcp-server) with 5 tools: verify-receipt, inspect-receipt, decode-receipt (pure, offline), issue-receipt, create-bundle (privileged, require issuer key). Works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, and custom agents.

### Trace (/trace)
Monitor agent traffic accessing your APIs and content. Export signed evidence. Preview.

### Proof Check (/agent-proof-check)
Paste logs, traces, webhooks, signed records, or incident summaries from AI agents, APIs, tool calls, and MCP servers. See what another party can verify, what is missing, and what holds up in a review, dispute, or audit. Runs in the browser. No signup.

### Declare (/declare)
Policy Pack Generator. Generate peac.txt, AIPREF headers, and human-readable policy pages from a single YAML config.

### CLI and SDKs (/downloads)
@peac/cli, @peac/mcp-server, @peac/capture-node, @peac/rails-stripe. npm and source archives. Requires Node 22+.

## Developer Resources

- [Developers](/developers) - Quickstart and integration guides
- [Downloads](/downloads) - CLI tools and install instructions
- [Demo](/demo) - Interactive protocol trace demonstration
- [Receipts](/receipts) - Verifiable interaction records documentation

## Integrations

- [MCP](/integrations/mcp) - Model Context Protocol: MCP security and governance via signed records
- [A2A](/integrations/a2a) - Agent-to-Agent communication with verifiable records
- [x402 / HTTP 402](/integrations/x402) - HTTP 402 payment evidence for machine-payable APIs
- [ACP](/integrations/acp) - Agentic Commerce Protocol evidence
- [AIPREF](/integrations/aipref) - AI Preferences signaling standard

## Open Source

PEAC is an open standard (Apache-2.0). 35 packages on npm, 7241 tests, 219 conformance requirement IDs, 96 build targets.

- Ed25519 cryptographic signing (RFC 8032)
- Compact JWS (RFC 7515)
- Policy discovery via /.well-known/peac.txt
- AI preferences via AIPREF (/.well-known/aipref.json)
- Self-hostable verification: does not require Originary to be online
- Offline verification with issuer public key only

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/peacprotocol/peac) - Source code, spec, and SDKs
- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/org/peac) - Published packages

## Technical Files

- [jwks.json](/.well-known/jwks.json)
- [sitemap.xml](/sitemap.xml)

## For AI Agents

This site supports PEAC Protocol. This llms.txt is provided as a convenience for inference-time tools and agents. Canonical verification comes from PEAC interaction record signatures and policy validation.

To interact programmatically:
1. Fetch /.well-known/peac.txt for policy discovery
2. Check /.well-known/aipref.json for usage preferences
3. Handle HTTP 402 responses with x402 or payment gateway
4. Verify interaction records via /.well-known/jwks.json

## Usage Guidelines

- Indexing: Permitted
- Training: Check AIPREF preferences
- RAG/Inference: Permitted with attribution
- Commercial use: Permitted under terms
- Attribution: Required for content reproduction
